chunk_summary: This section outlines the operational workflow for pediatric tele-nutrition prescribing, emphasizing regulatory compliance, decision-making steps, and digital tool integration to ensure safe and effective telemedicine practices in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the classification of teleconsultation types (first consult vs. follow-up) in pediatric tele-nutrition.
Learn the importance of modality selection (video vs. audio/text) for prescribing supplements and medications.
Identify the role of RMPs in authorizing therapeutic dosing and prescriptions in tele-nutrition.
Explore digital tool features for enforcing telemedicine safety filters and workflow compliance.
key_facts: Telemedicine rules require classification of visits as first consult or follow-up for safe prescribing.
Video modality is mandatory for higher-risk prescriptions; audio/text is limited to low-risk items.
Only Registered Medical Practitioners (RMPs) can authorize therapeutic dosing and prescriptions.
E-prescriptions must include clinician identity, registration number, patient details, drug name, dose, frequency, duration, and follow-up rules.
Telemedicine drug list logic restricts prohibited items and mandates safety filters for prescribing workflows.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Operationalize telemedicine rules by forcing three decisions before any therapeutic order is issued: visit type, modality, and prescriber authority. (Telemedicine Practice Guidelines, MoHFW, 2020)
